learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate knowledge and competency in management of a veterinary practice, including client care, employee safety and welfare, financial management and management of animals under your care.

Be able to explain the codes and regulations governing effective operation of a veterinary practice, including those related to animal care and protection, codes of practice, radiation safety and relevant drug legislations.

Prepare for clinical practice through engagement in industry related practical experiences, demonstrating practical capabilities in animal husbandry and handling, developing insight into the business management associated with animal enterprises.

Demonstrate a professional approach to working with and communicating with different Industry Partners and clients in animal enterprises, in operating as a multidisciplinary team, succinct to health and safety requirements and local risk factors.

assessments.task_description:
Reflective Task (A) Learning Plan will consist of a written reflection prepared by students individually before they commence the 5 day In Course Animal Husbandry Work Integrated Learning Placement (see Assessment Task #3). Details of this assessment task are published on the Course Blackboard site. 

If a student achieves less than 5/10 (50%) of the total assessment for this Reflective Task, they will be required to discuss their performance with the course coordinator (or delegate) and will be required to resubmit the task before commencement of the AHWIL placement. The maximum mark possible for such resubmission will be 5/10 or 50% of the total assessment for this reflective task.

assessments.assessment_title: In Course Animal Husbandry Work Integrated Learning Placement
assessments.task_description:
Performance Assessment

This within-course Animal Husbandry Work Integrated Learning (AHWIL) placement is designed to equip students for clinical placement in their 3rd year of the Veterinary Technology Program. The placement is the ultimate opportunity for students in their pre-clinic phase to demonstrate skills in animal husbandry, communication, professional attitudes and behaviours within veterinary contexts. For this assessment task, you will be required to attend 5 days of professional placement in a veterinary enterprise during the semester. 

This assessment task is built on prior learning and assessment, as follows:

  1. Completion of animal handling and professional training in pre-requisite courses for VETS2009, as outlined in the Course Information page of the Course Profile. 
  2. Successful completion of 20 days of preliminary AHWIL, as required under the program rules of the Bachelor of Veterinary Technology. Thus, the total number of days that must be completed to pass VETS2009 will be 25 days, including this in-course AHWIL.  
  3. Successful completion of Reflective Task A of the Assessment Sequence: Reflections on AHWIL, as outlined in the VETS2009 Course Profile. 

Credit applications for prior experience will not be considered for this work-integrated learning task.  

Student performance will be assessed via an assessment rubric, completed by providers of placement, in consultation with the Course Coordinator or their delegate. To pass this task, students must be graded as 'SATISFACTORY' in each of the five domains of activity on this placement.

Details of the rubric and other information can be found in the file entitled 'VETS2009 Industry Placement Resource', posted to the VETS2009 Blackboard site.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Professional Industry Partners will provide an assessment of student performance.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Professional Industry Partners will provide an assessment of student performance.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Reflections on AHWIL (B) Reflection on Learning
assessments.task_description:
Reflective Task Part B- Reflection of Learning will consist of an audio-visual reflection prepared by students individually on their experiences and learning arising directly from the 5-day In-Course Animal Husbandry Work Integrated Learning (AHWIL) undertaken as part of VETS2009. In a short recorded presentation of no more than 7 minutes duration, students will reflect specifically on the learning outcomes of the in-course placement task, noting how their learning and skills have developed as a result of the placement and specifically how they will advance these in later learning opportunities. Further information on this assessment task, including assessment criteria and rubric, can be found in the course document entitled 'VETS2009 Industry Placement Resource', published in the Course Blackboard site. 

If a student achieves less than 7.5/15 (50%) of total course assessment for this Reflective Task, they will be required to discuss their performance with the course coordinator or delegate and will be required to resubmit the task before the end of Semester. The maximum mark possible for such resubmission will be 7.5/15 (50%) of total mark for this reflective task.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 12(27 Jul - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Principles of Clinical Practice Lectures

A series of lectures that will guide students to develop knowledge and competency in management of veterinary practices. The Lectures will include:

Week 1: Indigenous Perspectives, Animal Care and Protection Act, The Code of Practice, Methods of Practice

Week 2: Self and Animal Advocacy, Veterinary Practice Organization and Management

Week 3: Veterinary Practice Organization, Operations and Management, Applied Ethical Practice

Week 4: Vet Reception Fundamentals, Introduction to Drug Legislation, Managing a Vet Pharmacy, Drug Terminology and Abbreviations

Week 5: Veterinary Practice Finances, HR Management and Healthy workplaces, Client Care and Customer Service

Week 6: Euthanasia, Mental Health Well-Being, Workplace Health and Safety

Week 7: Microchipping, Vet Practice Marketing, Third party Finance, Pet Insurance.

Week 8: Behaviour and Puppy Pre-School

Week 9: No Lectures Scheduled

Week 10: No Lectures Scheduled

Week 11: No Lectures Scheduled

Week 12: Registration as a Vet Technologist or Vet Nurse

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 6 To Mid Sem break
learning_activities.topic:
Animal Husbandry Work Integrated Learning (AHWIL) Placement

This 5-day placement must be completed before the end of week 10, with preference for activity during the mid-semester break. Students will attend 5-days of AHWIL placement. This activity will be assessed through two reflections and by the Industry Partner of the placement.

AHWIL is permitted in the mid-semester break and must be completed by 10 October 2026.
